purpose: *** THE DECLARATION OF CANONICAL AUTHORITY. For every canonical artifact, and every field class within it: who owns it, what regenerates it, what validates it, which manual edits are permitted, and — when two sources disagree — WHICH ONE WINS. ***

the_conflict_resolution_rule.rule: *** IMPLEMENTATION WINS OVER PROSE. *** For any field classified DERIVED_FROM_CODE, the code is authoritative and the declared artifact is WRONG when they disagree. There is no case in which a document overrides a measurement, unless an executable policy explicitly says so (and none does).
the_conflict_resolution_rule.why_this_rule_and_not_a_softer_one: This repository's signature defect — found in EVERY ONE of five audit cycles — is: 'THE DOC NAMES A MECHANISM THAT DOES NOT EXIST, WHILE THE PROPERTY SURVIVES VIA A DIFFERENT ONE.' It has appeared in the docs (INV-01/02/03/05/07), in the CODE COMMENTS (four for four), and — most dangerously — IN THE TEST LAYER (AR-03/RC-5, where a GREEN test asserted a data-loss outcome and called it correct). A governance system that let prose win would institutionalise that defect.
the_conflict_resolution_rule.the_proof_it_was_needed: Cycle 7's first run found the Cycle-6 contract pinning `_CLI_PRINT_COUNT = 147` as a load-bearing, exact-equality boundary. The test file says 158. The contract's copy went stale in ONE COMMIT.

field_classes.DERIVED_FROM_CODE: Mechanically extracted from src/. NEVER hand-written. Regenerated; byte-compared; the code wins.
field_classes.DERIVED_FROM_CONTRACT: Mechanically extracted from a DECLARED artifact or a CI test file. Regenerated; the SOURCE artifact wins.
field_classes.DECLARED_AND_ENFORCED: Human-authored, and a policy rule mechanically enforces it. Changing it is a reviewed act.
field_classes.GENERATED_FROM_SCHEMA: Structure fixed by a schema; contents derived.
field_classes.HUMAN_DECISION: A judgement no extractor can make. Cannot be regenerated. Its CONSISTENCY with derived facts is still checked.
field_classes.UNKNOWN: Cannot be established. Recorded in governance/unknowns.json. NEVER speculated.

artifacts.validation_mechanism: python -m tools.arch drift — regenerates into a temp dir and BYTE-COMPARES against the committed files. Rule ARCH-006.
artifacts.conflict_resolution: The generator wins, always. A hand-edit is destroyed by the next regen — which is the point.

fields.declared_by_the_ci_tests (ratchets.json): DERIVED_FROM_CONTRACT — parsed from tests/test_swallow_ratchet.py and tests/test_internal_prints_routed.py, which are the CANONICAL enforcement.
fields.contract_surface.json: DERIVED_FROM_CONTRACT — the Cycle-6 contract RESOLVED against the real AST. A declared boundary naming a function that does not exist is not a boundary, it is a wish.

artifacts.regeneration_command: NOT regenerable — the partition is a HUMAN_DECISION. Its TOTALITY is checked.
artifacts.validation_mechanism: Rules ARCH-001 (partition is total over the derived module set) + ARCH-002 (no ghosts) + ARCH-009 (its totality block matches the derived module count).
artifacts.permitted_manual_edits: Assign a module to a subsystem; add/rename a subsystem. Each is a reviewed change.
artifacts.conflict_resolution: The MODULE SET is derived and wins. The GROUPING is declared and stands. A module absent from the partition is a BLOCKING finding, not an inference.

fields.subsystems.<id>.modules: HUMAN_DECISION (the grouping) — but the DOMAIN of the map is DERIVED_FROM_CODE and enforced total.
fields.totality.*: DERIVED_FROM_CODE — a cached count. It rots. Rule ARCH-009 catches it. MIGRATION TARGET: delete these fields and point at derived/modules.json.
fields.subsystems.<id>.compile_depends_on: DERIVED_FROM_CODE — a cached projection of the import graph. MIGRATION TARGET.
fields.DEFINITION.*: HUMAN_DECISION — the definition of what a subsystem IS. Stands.

artifacts.status: CANONICAL DECLARED (Cycle 5) — but almost ENTIRELY derivable
artifacts.regeneration_command: python -m tools.arch regen  (the derived twin is derived/dependencies.json)
artifacts.validation_mechanism: Rule ARCH-009 cross-checks every numeric claim against derived/dependencies.json.
artifacts.permitted_manual_edits: The PROSE only (THE_THREE_GRAPHS, intentionality_SPLIT, the notes). Not one number.
artifacts.conflict_resolution: The derived graph WINS on every number.

fields.totals.*: DERIVED_FROM_CODE. MIGRATION TARGET — these numbers exist in two places and one of them will rot.
fields.THE_THREE_GRAPHS.*: HUMAN_DECISION — the semantic distinction between G1/G1c/G2/G3. This is the most valuable content in the file and it is NOT derivable. It stands.
fields.intentionality_SPLIT.*: HUMAN_DECISION + UNKNOWN — whether the mutual-import wiring is intentional is UNK-C5-4 and is NOT guessed.

artifacts.validation_mechanism: Rule ARCH-003 — every env var READ by the code must be declared here. An undeclared var is an undocumented input to a live system, and the operator is a DOCUMENTED hand-editor of .env (AR-09).
artifacts.permitted_manual_edits: Add a variable's SEMANTICS (what it does, its default, whether Studio-settable). Not its read sites.
artifacts.conflict_resolution: The code wins on WHICH vars exist and WHERE they are read. The doc wins on WHAT THEY MEAN.

fields.precedence_model.*: HUMAN_DECISION — Config vs Settings, the propagation asymmetry. Not derivable. Stands.

artifacts.validation_mechanism: Rule ARCH-008 cross-checks counts_AST_verified against derived/side_effects.json.
artifacts.permitted_manual_edits: The DECISION TABLE (the network-ambiguity table) and the ordering narrative. Not the counts.
artifacts.conflict_resolution: The code wins on counts. The doc wins on SEMANTICS (what a 5xx MEANS for a publish).

fields.the_network_ambiguity_decision_table.*: HUMAN_DECISION — the system's best engineering, and entirely non-derivable. Stands.
fields.the_publish_side_effect_ordering.*: HUMAN_DECISION — the T0..T10 ordering and the crash-critical window.

artifacts.validation_mechanism: PARTIAL. IMPL-009 (GB-4) and IMPL-010 (GB-3) mechanize two. The rest are prose.
artifacts.permitted_manual_edits: All of it — but a claim that an invariant is MECHANICALLY enforced must name the mechanism, and the mechanism must exist.
artifacts.conflict_resolution: The code wins. SEVEN invariants in this file are already recorded as FALSE AS WRITTEN, which is the file working as intended.

fields.invariants[].enforcement: DECLARED_AND_ENFORCED — the enforcement CLASS is the load-bearing field. 'MECHANICAL_TEST' is a checkable claim: the test must exist and its ASSERTION must be read, not its name (AR-03).
fields.invariants_that_are_FALSE_as_written: HUMAN_DECISION — and the most valuable section in the KB.

artifacts.validation_mechanism: Rules IMPL-001 (every owned file exists) + IMPL-002 (every permitted_functions entry resolves to a real function). derived/contract_surface.json is its resolved twin.
artifacts.permitted_manual_edits: Ownership and boundaries — each a REVIEWED scope change.
artifacts.conflict_resolution: The FILE SET is checked against disk. A boundary naming a function that does not exist is a BLOCKING finding.

fields.ownership.<file>.partition.<slice>.permi: *** DECLARED_AND_ENFORCED — BUT CURRENTLY PARTLY PROSE. *** Entries like 'the daemon tick loop (:1300-1313)' are NOT machine-checkable. Rule IMPL-002 reports them. MIGRATION TARGET: rewrite as bare function identifiers.
fields.ownership.<file>.ratchets: DERIVED_FROM_CONTRACT — a COPY of the CI test baselines. Rule IMPL-007 checks the copy against the original. It has ALREADY rotted once.

artifacts.validation_mechanism: Rules IMPL-003 (DAG acyclic, PROVEN by Tarjan not asserted), IMPL-007 (ratchet copies), IMPL-008 (every slice traces to a root cause).
artifacts.conflict_resolution: Derived facts win over the contract's copies of them.

fields.implementation_dag.acyclic: DERIVED_FROM_CONTRACT — this is an ASSERTION in the file and a PROOF in derived/contract_surface.json. The proof wins.
fields.GLOBAL_BOUNDARIES.GB-6_ast_ratchet_budge: *** DERIVED_FROM_CONTRACT — a COPY of the CI test files. IT IS STALE TODAY (contract says _CLI_PRINT_COUNT=147; the test says 158). Rule IMPL-007. MIGRATION TARGET: delete the copy, point at derived/ratchets.json. ***
fields.live_state_at_contract_freeze.*: *** HUMAN_DECISION + EVIDENCE_DECAY. These are measurements of a MUTABLE live tree (LS-1..LS-7). They are TRUE AS OF 2026-07-14 and they are NOT re-derivable from the repository at all — the live ledger is not in git. They MUST be re-run at merge (IR-11), and this system CANNOT do it for you. ***
fields.product_decisions.PD-*: HUMAN_DECISION. PD-3 and PD-5 BLOCK slices. No extractor can answer them and none tries.

artifacts.regeneration_command: python -m tools.arch baseline --accept  (a DELIBERATE, REVIEWED act — never a side effect of regen)
artifacts.validation_mechanism: Rules ARCH-004 (no new compile cycle), ARCH-007 (no lazy import hoisted), IMPL-009 (no fifth door to a terminal Post state).
artifacts.permitted_manual_edits: None directly — accept a new baseline via the command, and EXPLAIN WHY in the PR.
artifacts.conflict_resolution: The baseline is the RATCHET. Code that violates it is the thing that is wrong, until a human deliberately re-accepts.

fields.must_stay_lazy: DECLARED_AND_ENFORCED — mechanizes GB-1, which NOTHING enforced before Cycle 7.
fields.approved_compile_cycles: DECLARED_AND_ENFORCED — baselined because it EXISTS, not because it is endorsed (UNK-C5-1).

artifacts.validation_mechanism: Regenerated in CI; a diff means the doc is stale.
artifacts.permitted_manual_edits: NONE. It is a VIEW of the machine artifacts, never a source.
artifacts.conflict_resolution: The artifacts win. Editing this file is pointless — the next `docs` run overwrites it.

what_this_system_CANNOT_authorize.note: *** Stated plainly, so no future agent mistakes silence for coverage. ***
what_this_system_CANNOT_authorize.live_state: The Cycle-6 contract's LS-1..LS-7 measurements (0 stranded posts, 0 retired moments, 0 malformed backends, 41 shrink dirs, 73 published records, 11 snapshot artifacts) are properties of the OPERATOR'S LIVE TREE. They are NOT IN GIT. This system cannot re-derive them, cannot detect their decay, and does not pretend to. They are re-armed as MERGE GATES (IR-11) and a human must re-run them.
what_this_system_CANNOT_authorize.product_decisions: PD-1..PD-5. Not recoverable from code. Not guessed.
what_this_system_CANNOT_authorize.intent: UNK-C5-1 (is the persona import cycle intentional?) and UNK-C5-2 (how many of the 56 strictly-upward lazy imports are deliberate?) are UNKNOWN. Cycle 7 PINS them so they cannot get worse. It does not resolve them.
what_this_system_CANNOT_authorize.the_runtime_call_graph: G3 was never derived by any cycle. G2 is a strict SUPERSET of it. Every SCC claim in this KB BOUNDS blast radius; it does not ESTABLISH it.
